hints.Neminem: 'Neminem' is a pronoun meaning 'no one' in the accusative — the direct object
hints.nemo: 'nemo' is the nominative form of the pronoun — 'Neminem' is the accusative used here
hints.concutere: 'concutere' is the infinitive — 'concutiatis' is the conjugated form

hints.to cut: 'to cut' is 'secare/caedere' — 'concutiatis' comes from 'concutere' (to shake violently)
hints.to strike down: 'to strike down' is 'percutere' — 'concutere' means 'to shake violently/extort'

hints.Indicative: Indicative present would be 'concutitis' — 'concutiatis' with -ia- is subjunctive
hints.Imperative: Imperative would be 'concutite' — 'concutiatis' is a subjunctive prohibition
hints.Infinitive: Infinitive would be 'concutere' — 'concutiatis' is a conjugated subjunctive

hints.quatio, quatere, quassi, quassum: These are the parts of 'quatere' (to shake) — 'concutere' adds the prefix con-
hints.percutio, percutere, percussi, percussum: These are the parts of 'percutere' (to strike through)
hints.discutio, discutere, discussi, discussum: These are the parts of 'discutere' (to shatter/discuss)

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'nemo' — 'Neminem' with -em ending is accusative
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'nemini' — 'Neminem' is accusative, direct object of 'concutiatis'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'neminis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'neminis' ends in -is
hints.Indeclinable: 'nemo' is fully declinable (nemo, neminis, nemini, neminem, nemine) — 3rd declension
